auth.login_prompt: Please enter the admin password to complete login
auth.connect_failed: Cannot connect to panel service, please refresh and try again
auth.session_expired: Login session expired, please login again

common.request_failed: Request failed, please try again later
common.request_timeout: Request wait timeout, please refresh the history later to check if it completed.
common.service_busy: Panel service busy, please try again later
common.service_exception: Panel service returned exception ({{status}}), please check if the service is running or refresh and retry
common.network_error: Cannot connect to panel service. Please check if the panel is running, network connection, HTTPS certificate, or access entry is correct, then refresh and retry.

dashboard.site_resources_note: Aggregates CPU and memory usage across all processes for each site by scanning the system user of each site's PHP-FPM processes. Refresh interval 30 seconds.

alert.rule_remote_backup: Remote backup failed (remote backup must be enabled first)
alert.rate_limit_help: Alerts are sent at most once every 30 minutes. Recovery notification will be sent when the issue resolves.

extension.reset_confirm: Will restore to default theme and plugin list, current configuration will be overwritten. Confirm?
extension.description: Default optional theme and plugin list for new WordPress sites. Enabled items will be pre-checked on the site creation page.
extension.name: Name

cron.wp_cron_replace_help: WordPress by default only triggers scheduled tasks on page visits (publishing scheduled posts, checking for updates, etc.), which is unstable and consumes performance. This task directly calls wp-cron.php via system Cron for more reliable scheduling.
cron.wp_cron_config_help_prefix: After creation, will automatically add to wp-config.php
cron.wp_cron_config_help_suffix: to disable WordPress built-in pseudo Cron. When deleting this task (and the site has no other WP Cron tasks), this configuration will be automatically removed.
cron.full_backup_help: : Archives the entire website root directory. Runs on first execution or when triggered from the details page.
cron.smart_incremental_help: : Only backs up files in wp-content/uploads/ that have been added or modified since the last backup. Recommended for scheduled daily runs.
cron.incremental_first_full: Smart Incremental (auto full on first run)
cron.incremental_keep_help: Incremental backups always keep the latest complete chain locally, not auto-cleaned.
cron.weekly_sunday_3am: Weekly on Sunday at 3 AM (every 7 days)
cron.cron_expression_placeholder: Or enter standard Cron expression (minute hour day month weekday)

software.php_size_invalid: PHP capacity configuration only supports numbers, or numbers with K/M/G suffix, e.g. 128M
software.php_int_invalid: PHP time and variable count configuration only supports non-negative integers
software.nginx_value_no_semicolon: Nginx configuration value cannot contain semicolons
software.create_php_config_failed: Failed to create PHP configuration file
software.php_pool_rebuild_failed: PHP configuration written, but some site PHP-FPM Pool rebuilds failed: {{error}}
software.max_input_time_hint: Maximum time for PHP to parse POST/upload input. Panel default is 300 seconds; for large file uploads or imports, consider matching max_execution_time.
software.memory_limit_hint: Maximum memory per PHP process. Simple blog 128M, multi-plugin site 256M, WooCommerce/Elementor 512M.
software.upload_max_filesize_hint: Theme, plugin, media upload limit. Should match Nginx client_max_body_size.
software.post_max_size_hint: Should be ≥ upload_max_filesize, otherwise large file uploads will be blocked by POST limit.
software.max_execution_time_hint: Maximum PHP script runtime. Demo data import/batch processing recommended 300+.
software.max_input_vars_hint: Many menu items or Elementor/Divi use recommended 2000+, large sites 5000.
software.client_max_body_size_hint: Must match PHP upload_max_filesize. Increase when importing large themes or backups.
software.innodb_buffer_pool_size_hint: Conservative recommendation: 10%~25% of physical memory. 1G set to 128M, 2G set to 256M, 4G set to 512M, 8G+ set to 1G+.
software.maxmemory_hint: Redis object cache limit. WordPress single site 128mb, multi-site or high traffic 256mb+.
software.process_guard_help: systemd Restart=always provides first-layer guarding; the panel checks every 30 seconds and auto-restarts on anomalies. After manual stop, the guard no longer auto-starts.

ai_diagnostics.description: After selecting a website and issue type, initiates read-only diagnostics; will not automatically modify files, databases, or server configuration.
ai_diagnostics.privacy_notice: Diagnostic context is sent to the AI service provider configured in panel settings, which may include domain, log summaries, configuration summaries, and recent operations; it will not send database passwords, AI keys, SSL private keys, panel passwords, or panel database content.
ai_diagnostics.timeout_help: If the page times out, the diagnosis may still be processing in the background; you can refresh the history later to view results.
ai_diagnostics.feedback_help: If you encounter inaccurate answers, incorrect panel entry information, or clearly unreasonable judgments, please send a diagnostic screenshot or problem description to provide feedback.
ai_diagnostics.select_site_for_history: Please select a website to view history
ai_diagnostics.results_placeholder: Diagnosis results will be displayed here
ai_diagnostics.needs_more_info: AI considers the current information insufficient; please provide additional information based on the above suggestions, then continue the conversation below.
ai_diagnostics.followup_help: After sending, the current site status will be re-collected with this conversation context.
ai_diagnostics.empty_chat_help: After diagnosis is complete, you can describe what you did or paste new error messages below.
ai_diagnostics.followup_placeholder: Example: I have enabled debug mode, please re-check the current status.
ai_diagnostics.followup_context_help: Continuing the conversation preserves the original diagnosis and historical Q&A; no need to initiate a new diagnosis.
ai_diagnostics.waiting_long: Wait time is long, please continue to be patient
ai_diagnostics.followup_wait: Please wait before sending additional questions
ai_diagnostics.user_error_network: Network error connecting to AI service

files.remote_import_allow_insecure_tls: Allow self-signed or untrusted HTTPS certificates
files.file_lock_notice: Current WordPress site has file lock enabled. Media uploads, cache, translations, and security plugin logs can continue writing; core, plugins, themes, site configuration, and PHP executable file writes will be blocked.
files.remote_import_filename_required: Cannot identify filename from URL, please enter filename manually
files.remote_import_task_missing: Remote import task does not exist
files.remote_import_create_file_failed: Failed to create remote import file: {{error}}
files.remote_import_disk_space_low: Target disk has less than 1GB remaining, download stopped
files.remote_import_completed_fix_permissions: Remote import complete, permission setting failed, please click Fix Permissions
files.remote_import_url_no_userinfo: Remote URL cannot contain username or password
files.remote_import_host_local: Remote URL cannot point to local address
files.remote_import_host_private: Remote URL cannot point to internal network or local address
files.remote_import_too_many_redirects: Too many redirects in remote address
files.target_is_directory: Target path already exists and is a directory
files.name: Name
files.confirm_fix_permissions:
Confirm fix file permissions for {{domain}}?

Directories will be set to 755, files to 644, sensitive files will have tightened permissions, owner set to site user:site user
files.paste_target_required: Please select target website or directory first
files.skip_existing_prompt: Skip existing items and continue pasting other items?
files.compression_busy: Compressing, may take some time for large files, please wait for completion notification
files.decompression_busy: Extracting, may take some time for large files, please wait for completion notification
files.extract_overwrite_conflict:
The following files will be overwritten, confirm continue?

{{items}}

security.rate_limit_help: When enabled, unlogged visitors are subject to rate limiting; logged-in WordPress users are automatically exempt. Multiple 429 triggers will be banned by Fail2ban
security.requests_per_minute_help: Non-Elementor heavy pages recommended 30-60. Elementor/multi-plugin sites recommended 60-120. See top-right "Help" for adjustment suggestions and testing methods
security.burst_help: Concurrent request buffer for initial page load. Elementor/multi-plugin sites recommended 150-300, non-Elementor recommended 30-120. See top-right "Help" for adjustment suggestions and testing methods
security.burst_warning: Note: IPs that trigger multiple 429s will be auto-banned by Fail2ban, please ensure sufficient buffer
security.enable_bot_limit: Enable Bot UA Unified Rate Limiting
security.bot_limit_help: Disabled by default. When enabled, each site has independent rate limiting; common Bot UAs within the same site share that site's rate limit bucket, normal browsers are unaffected.
security.bot_requests_help: Default 30. Used to suppress multi-IP fake crawlers continuously scanning sites, preventing WordPress dynamic 404 exhaustion.
security.bot_burst_help: Default 20. Googlebot/Bingbot are exempt only when source IP belongs to official ranges; fake search crawlers enter the rate limit bucket.
security.custom_whitelist: Custom Whitelist (not overwritten by official fetch)
security.wp_security_path_help: One path per line, wildcards * supported; must start with /. Regex not supported, no spaces, semicolons, quotes, or .. allowed.
security.cdn_realip_subtitle: Fill in once, use in multiple places
security.cdn_realip_conclusion: Conclusion: After enabling CDN Real IP for a website, log analysis, suspicious access identification, and auto-banning will work based on real visitor IPs.
security.cdn_realip_compatible_help: When you don't know how to fill in CDN origin IP ranges, select "General CDN (Compatible Mode)". Normal CDN scenarios will not accidentally ban CDN nodes.
security.cdn_realip_strict_help: When you can obtain CDN origin IP ranges, adding a configuration group and filling them in can reduce the risk of Header spoofing. Cloudflare is handled automatically by the system.
security.compatible_group_help: Select this mode when you don't know how to fill in CDN origin IP ranges, suitable for most normal CDN scenarios.
security.cloudflare_group_help: Cloudflare real IP is automatically handled globally by the system; site details pages do not need to select this group.
security.name: Name
security.trusted_cdn_origin_ips_placeholder: One per line, leave empty for compatible mode
security.trusted_cdn_origin_ips_help: Leave empty when you don't know how to fill in CDN origin IP ranges; site will use compatible mode. Fill in when you can obtain vendor origin IP ranges for stricter, more reliable logs and banning.
security.description_placeholder: Used to describe CDN vendor or purpose
security.header_name_help: Common China CDNs typically use X-Forwarded-For; Header names only allow letters, numbers, and hyphens.
security.cdn_mode_strict_trusted_ips: Strict Mode: Trusted origin IP ranges filled
security.cdn_mode_compatible_no_origin_ips: Compatible Mode: Used when you don't know origin IP ranges
security.cdn_mode_compatible_missing_origin_ips: Compatible Mode: Trusted origin IP ranges not filled
security.telemetry_help: Reports anonymous heartbeat once daily to stats.wp-panel.org, containing only machine anonymous identifier (SHA256 hash of /etc/machine-id) and panel version number. No IP, domain, website information, or any sensitive data is included. This helps us know how many users support WP Panel.
security.fetch_cdn_groups_failed: Failed to read CDN configuration groups
security.confirm_delete_cdn_group: Confirm delete CDN configuration group "{{name}}"? Bound websites will no longer use this group.
security.telemetry_disable_confirm:
After disabling, your server installation will no longer be counted.

Reported content absolutely does not contain any sensitive information — only machine anonymous identifier (SHA256 hash) and panel version number.
This simply lets us know how many users support WP Panel.

Confirm disable?

firewall.ip_filled_notice: IP has been filled into the manual ban IP box, please manually select ban duration and click the ban button
firewall.load_wp_report_failed: Failed to read WordPress suspicious access analysis: {{error}}
firewall.refresh_file_security_failed: Failed to refresh file security events: {{error}}
firewall.confirm_clear_history: Confirm clear history events and rescan?
firewall.file_security_summary: File security summary: {{sites}} sites, {{files}} file events, {{access}} access events
firewall.copy_failed_manual: Copy failed, please select content manually
firewall.wp_suspicious_access_help: Based on local WordPress security logs and Nginx error logs, provides evidence compilation only, no auto-banning.
firewall.wp_suspicious_access_cache_help: Analysis results cached for 30 seconds; when WordPress sites exceed 50 or disk I/O is slow, the first refresh may be slower.
firewall.file_security_events_help: Records PHP execution attempts and suspicious PHP files discovered in runtime data directories; display and alert only, no auto-banning or file deletion.

website.cdn_header_mismatch: Selected configuration group headers are inconsistent, save will be rejected.
website.cdn_compatible_no_origin_ips: Compatible Mode: Trusted origin IP ranges not filled, will trust all real IP headers.
website.cdn_strict_origin_ips: Trusted CDN Mode: Only trusts the selected configuration group's origin IP ranges.
website.cdn_select_non_cloudflare_required: Please select at least one non-Cloudflare CDN configuration group; Cloudflare is auto-handled by the system and does not need saving.
website.ssl_manual_required: Please fill in certificate content and private key
website.confirm_delete_ssl: Confirm delete SSL certificate for {{domain}}? After deletion, HTTP access will be restored.
website.sync_wp_config_base: Will overwrite DB_NAME and DB_USER in wp-config.php with current database name and username
website.detect_multiple_prefixes: Multiple possible prefixes detected: {{candidates}}. Pre-selected "{{prefix}}", if inaccurate please modify manually
website.detect_prefix_found: Table prefix detected: {{prefix}}, please click "Sync Database Info" to save
website.confirm_clear_database: Confirm clear database "{{db}}"? This will delete all tables and cannot be undone!
website.load_log_files_failed: Failed to load log file list: {{error}}
website.log_empty_or_missing: (Log is empty or file does not exist)
website.confirm_restore_backup: Will overwrite current database with {{filename}}, this operation cannot be undone. Confirm restore?
website.confirm_restore_upload: Will overwrite current database with {{filename}}, confirm restore?
website.restore_still_running: Restore task still running, please refresh the page later to check results
website.file_lock_enable_confirm: After enabling file lock, WordPress can still publish posts, upload media, generate cache, and write plugin runtime logs; but core, plugin, theme, and site configuration modifications will be blocked. Please temporarily disable lock before installing/updating plugins/themes or first configuring security/cache plugins. Confirm enable?
website.file_lock_disable_confirm: After disabling file lock, WordPress backend and panel file manager can again modify code and site configuration. Confirm disable?
website.new_password_label: New Password (leave empty for random generation)
website.new_password_placeholder: Leave empty for auto-generated 24-character random password
website.auto_ssl_help: Will auto-request free SSL certificate via Let's Encrypt. Please ensure the domain is correctly pointed to this server.
website.reinstall_warning: ⚠ This operation will delete all website files, clear the database, and deploy a fresh WordPress installation. All data is unrecoverable.
website.php_password_help: For generic PHP sites, please set and record the password yourself. If forgotten, you can obtain a new password via the site details page.
website.document_root_help: For programs with entry point at public/index.php like Laravel, select public; for ordinary PHP programs, select project root.
website.expiry_help: Leave empty for no expiry. Alert emails sent 14/7/1 days before expiry.
website.enable_free_ssl: Also request free Let's Encrypt SSL certificate
website.remove_unused_default_themes: Delete unused default themes (Twenty Twenty-Four / Twenty Twenty-Three)
website.no_enabled_themes: No enabled themes, go to "Extension Configuration" to add
website.no_enabled_plugins: No enabled plugins, go to "Extension Configuration" to add
website.cloudflare_realip_auto: Cloudflare real IP is auto-handled by the system, no need to select here.
website.non_cloudflare_cdn_notice: This is only for Alibaba Cloud ESA, Tencent EdgeOne, Upyun, or other non-Cloudflare CDNs.
website.no_non_cloudflare_cdn_groups: No non-Cloudflare CDN configuration groups available, please add in Security Settings first.
website.document_root_detail_help: Site root directory is still used for file management and backup; this only changes the Nginx external access root.
website.monitoring_enabled_help: Periodically checks website HTTP status code; sends alert notification email on anomaly. SMTP must be configured in "Alert Notifications" first.
website.monitoring_disabled_help: When enabled, periodically checks if the website is accessible; email notification on anomaly.
website.table_prefix_help: After migration, if the database table prefix differs from wp-config.php, you can modify it here. Click "Auto Detect" to read the actual prefix from the database.
website.allow_plugin_read_ssl: Allow companion plugin to read SSL certificate
website.location_level_config: Location-level config (.conf — before location block, cache bypass rules go here)
website.file_lock_help: When enabled, WordPress can still publish posts and upload media normally; plugins, themes, core files, and code directory modifications will be blocked.
website.debug_mode_help: When enabled, PHP errors and warnings are written to wp-content/debug.log. Enable temporarily for troubleshooting website issues, disable for normal use to prevent log file growth.
website.post_revisions_help: Leave empty = WordPress default (unlimited), 0 = no revisions kept, recommended 3~5 to reduce database usage
website.memory_limit_help: e.g. 128M, 256M, increase when encountering memory exhaustion errors (white screen/500)
website.xmlrpc_help: Disabled by default; when enabled /xmlrpc.php becomes accessible
website.file_lock_enabled_notice: Current site is locked. To save optimizations that modify wp-config.php or install/update the companion plugin, please disable lock first.
website.fastcgi_cache_point_static: Nginx caches PHP page output as static HTML on disk; visitors get HTML directly without going through PHP/MariaDB
website.fastcgi_cache_point_key: Each site has an independent cache version key; click "Clear Cache" to rotate key, old cache files auto-deleted after 60 minutes
website.fastcgi_cache_point_wp_skip: Logged-in users, POST requests, wp-admin backend automatically bypass cache
website.fastcgi_cache_point_php_skip: POST requests and requests with query parameters automatically bypass cache
website.wp_optimizer_plugin_help: After installing the companion plugin (WP Panel Optimizer), site admins can manage cache, disable update detection, file editing, and other optimizations from the WordPress backend, syncing in real-time with panel settings. Do not install third-party cache plugins (WP Rocket / W3 Total Cache Nginx mode, etc.) as they will conflict with this card.
website.delete_confirm: Confirm delete website {{domain}}? This operation is irreversible.
website.log_preview_help: This area only previews the latest lines of the current log file, default last 200 lines; for full daily logs and historical rotated logs, please download for analysis.
website.cdn_select_non_cloudflare_help: This is only for Alibaba Cloud ESA, Tencent EdgeOne, Upyun, or other non-Cloudflare CDNs.

settings.basic_auth_desc: First layer of authentication for the random path entry, browser popup login
settings.password_keep: Password (at least 8 characters, leave empty to keep unchanged)
settings.web_login_desc: Web form login displayed after BasicAuth
settings.account_saved_please_relogin: Account settings saved, logging out now
settings.github_proxy_help: Fill in when China servers cannot access GitHub. Leave empty for direct GitHub connection.
settings.update_do_not_close: Do not close or refresh the page during update.
settings.auto_update_help: Disabled by default; when enabled, executes according to window, release delay, and signature verification
settings.system_update_help: Not sure what a package does? Click the "Query" link to view Debian's official package description, or copy the package name and use a search engine/AI tool for details.
settings.system_update_warning: The update process may take several minutes. Do not close or refresh the page during this time.
settings.ai_settings_help: Configure the AI service provider for website troubleshooting and performance diagnostics; diagnostic operations are performed on the "AI Diagnostics" page.
settings.ai_timeout_help: Recommended 60-120 seconds; slower models may temporarily increase to 180 seconds.
settings.ai_privacy_notice: AI diagnostics sends site domain, log summaries, configuration summaries, and check results to the selected service provider; it will not send database passwords, AI keys, SSL private keys, panel passwords, or panel database content.
settings.wp_package_help: When creating WordPress sites, local packages are used first. If China servers cannot download online, you can manually upload here.
settings.local_package_missing: No local package; site creation will attempt online download (may fail due to network issues)
settings.ssh_key_generated_help: Panel has auto-generated ed25519 key pair; public key will be embedded in the one-click command below.
settings.remote_disabled_help: When enabled, each backup completion auto-syncs to remote location
settings.remote_helper_create_help: Run the following command as root on the remote server to auto-create backup account and directory:
settings.remote_helper_delete_help: Run as root on the remote server; will delete account and backup directory:
settings.copy_to_remote: Copy to remote server to execute:
settings.remote_helper_key_mode: · Key authentication mode: command already includes public key, one paste to complete
settings.remote_helper_password_mode: · Password authentication mode: panel has auto-installed sshpass, no manual action needed
settings.remote_helper_dedicated_user: · Recommended to use a dedicated backup account, not root
settings.remote_helper_permission: · Backup account does not need admin privileges, only write access to target directory
settings.s3_help_title: S3 Compatible Object Storage Configuration Tips
settings.s3_help_supported: · Supports Cloudflare R2, AWS S3, MinIO, Backblaze B2, and other S3 compatible services.
settings.s3_help_bucket: · Create a Bucket in object storage, and create an Access Key with object read/write permissions.
settings.s3_help_prefix: · Path prefix used to distinguish different panels, e.g.
settings.s3_help_multipart: · Large backup files automatically use S3 multipart upload; restore still results in a complete backup object.
settings.workflow_local_backup: Panel completes database or file backup locally
settings.workflow_transfer: Auto-transfers backup files to remote location via SSH + rsync or S3 compatible API
settings.workflow_delete_local: If "Keep Local Backup" is disabled, local files are auto-deleted after successful transfer to save space
settings.remote_req_ssh: SSH service enabled and accessible from this machine
settings.remote_req_path: Target directory exists and backup account has write permissions
settings.auth_key_detail: SSH Key (Recommended): Panel auto-generates key pair; the one-click command in the helper includes the public key — paste and execute on remote. No additional software needed.
settings.auth_password_detail: Password: Panel auto-installs sshpass when saving settings, no manual action needed.
settings.auth_s3_detail: S3 Compatible Object Storage: Uses Access Key and Secret Key signed upload, no remote SSH account needed; large files auto-multipart upload.
settings.security_dedicated: Create a dedicated backup account on the remote server, do not use root; minimum privileges are more secure
settings.security_cleanup: Remote backup directory does not auto-clean old files; recommend regular manual checks
settings.remote_help_tip: Tip: The one-click setup commands generated by the helper on the right can be directly pasted into a remote server SSH terminal and executed — press Enter to complete all configuration.
settings.panel_db_backup_help: Auto-backs up panel database daily at 2:30 AM, retains the 7 most recent copies. Panel will auto-restart after restore.
settings.panel_db_backup_warning: ⚠ Backups contain sensitive data (password hashes, database credentials, etc.), do not share with others. If the panel cannot start, you can obtain backup files via SSH from /www/server/panel/backups/panel-db/.
settings.confirm_delete_local_wp_package: Confirm delete local WordPress installation package? Site creation will auto-download online after deletion.
settings.current_password_required: Current password required to change web password
settings.proxy_required: Please fill in reverse proxy address first
settings.panel_restarting: Panel is restarting or temporarily unreachable...
settings.update_status_timeout: Update status query timed out, please refresh the page to check panel status.
settings.confirm_update_version: Confirm update to {{version}}? Panel will be temporarily unavailable during update.
settings.update_completed_refresh: Update complete, panel restarting, auto-refreshing in 3 seconds...
settings.confirm_restore_db_backup:
Confirm restore database with this backup?

Panel will create a safety backup before restoring, and will auto-restart after restore.
If panel cannot start after restore, the safety backup can be used to roll back.
settings.command_copied: Command copied, paste to remote server and execute as root
settings.confirm_system_update:
Confirm execute system update?

Update may take several minutes. If the page times out, do not click repeatedly — background is still executing, repeated operations will cause dpkg lock conflicts.
